data.title: Fill code coverage gaps toward 95% line / 88% branch

epic.spec:
# Fill code coverage gaps toward 95% line / 88% branch

## Overview

The merged multi-TFM coverage report at `artifacts/ci-coverage-report/Summary.txt` (2026-04-10) shows 91.3% line / 80.4% branch / 71.3% method across 874 classes in three assemblies (`Humanizer`, `Humanizer.Analyzers`, `Humanizer.SourceGenerators`). The user's goal is "nearly 100%" with trivial property getters/setters omitted. This epic closes the genuine-logic gaps and excludes trivial T4-generated FluentDate sugar, then enforces the result as a CI gate.

Baseline work already in place: xUnit v3 + Microsoft Testing Platform, `UseCultureAttribute`, Verify snapshots, `CSharpAnalyzerTest<,,>` / `CSharpCodeFixTest<,,,>` verifiers, `CSharpGeneratorDriver` pattern in source-generator tests, `testconfig.json` listing attribute exclusions. PR #1715 deliberately kept generated YAML tables in coverage — preserve that direction.

## Scope

**In scope**
- Add a repo-level `coverlet.runsettings` (or `.runsettings` for `dotnet-coverage`) that excludes the T4-generated FluentDate files and any assembly excluded from the gate.
- Emit `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]` from the FluentDate `.tt` templates and regenerate the committed `.cs` outputs.
- Author real tests for the ten gap buckets enumerated below.
- Wire a hard coverage gate in `azure-pipelines.yml`, phased: freeze-at-baseline first, then raise to 95/88/85 in the final task.
- Update `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `docs/adding-a-locale.md` with the new test command and exclusion conventions.

**Out of scope**
- Stryker.NET mutation testing (advisory signal — file a separate epic).
- Coverage work for Urdu tests and for locales owned by fn-8.12–14 (cs, pl, ru, ar, he, hi) — wait for fn-8 to close.
- Any `Benchmarks/` project coverage.
- API additions or behavior changes. Tests and exclusion configuration only.

## Approach

Follow established patterns — no new test infrastructure needed.

- **Trivial DSL exclusion.** Edit `src/Humanizer/FluentDate/*.tt` templates to emit `[System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExcludeFromCodeCoverage(Justification = "Generated trivial DateTime factories")]` at the type level. Regenerate the `.cs` outputs (the repo commits them). `testconfig.json` already lists `ExcludeFromCodeCoverageAttribute`, so coverlet/dotnet-coverage will pick them up. No parametric theories — the members are verified trivial (`branch-rate=1`, single-expression).
- **Default fallback converters.** Instantiate `DefaultDateToOrdinalWordConverter`, `DefaultDateOnlyToOrdinalWordConverter`, `DefaultNumberToWordsConverter` directly in new test files under `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/Default/`. Use `CultureInfo.GetCultureInfo("ar-SA")` to exercise the mark-stripping branches (LTR/RTL/ALM) without touching the registry.
- **Registry default-factory path.** Call each converter through `Configurator.*` under a `UseCulture("xx-XX")` scope — an unknown culture forces the registry's default-factory lambda to build the `Default*` instance.
- **Branch-level gaps.** For each class listed in the buckets, read the Cobertura XML for that file, identify the uncovered lines/branches, and author targeted `[Theory]` or `[Fact]` inputs. No Verify snapshots for branch coverage — they run code but can hide branches.
- **Analyzer duality.** `Humanizer.Analyzers` has `#if ROSLYN_4_14_OR_GREATER` sections. Verify both arms compile and get instrumented. If the test matrix only compiles one, add a secondary test project conditionally referencing the older Roslyn, or mark the untested arm clearly.
- **Source generator error branches.** Author synthetic YAML fixtures under `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/Fixtures/` and feed them via `AdditionalText` to `CSharpGeneratorDriver`. Each fixture targets one diagnostic code path (unknown key, missing required surface, conflicting profile, malformed scalar).
- **Gate rollout.** Final task lands gate in `azure-pipelines.yml` using `dotnet test -- --coverage --coverage-settings coverage.runsettings`. Two-phase: freeze at current absolute numbers first (no regression), then raise to 95/88/85 after green.

## Quick commands

```bash
# Run the existing full suite with coverage
dotnet test tests/Humanizer.Tests/Humanizer.Tests.csproj --framework net10.0 -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura --xunit-info

# Regenerate merged report locally (requires ReportGenerator CLI)
reportgenerator -reports:"**/*.cobertura.xml" -targetdir:artifacts/local-coverage -reporttypes:"TextSummary;Html;Cobertura"

# Inspect a single file's uncovered lines from the merged Cobertura
python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; \
  t=ET.parse('artifacts/report-merged/Cobertura.xml'); \
  [print(l.get('number'), l.get('hits'), l.get('branch','')) \
   for c in t.iter('class') if 'YourClassName' in c.get('filename','') \
   for l in c.iter('line') if l.get('hits')=='0']"
```

## Acceptance

- [ ] `coverlet.runsettings` (or equivalent `.runsettings`) is checked into the repo and wired into `dotnet test` invocations in `azure-pipelines.yml`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `docs/adding-a-locale.md`.
- [ ] FluentDate T4 templates emit `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]`; regenerated `.cs` outputs committed; `Humanizer.In`, `Humanizer.InDate`, `Humanizer.On`, `Humanizer.OnDate` and their nested types disappear from the coverage report.
- [ ] All three `Default*` fallback converters reach ≥95% line and ≥90% branch coverage.
- [ ] `HeadingTableCatalog.Invariant` path is exercised by a real test (no `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]`).
- [ ] `TokenMapWordsToNumberOrdinalMapBuilder`, `WordsToNumberExtension.TryToNumber` overloads, `SuffixScaleWordsToNumberConverter`, `OrdinalDatePattern`, `LocalePhraseTable`, `DelimitedCollectionFormatter`, `CliticCollectionFormatter`, `NoMatchFoundException` all reach ≥90% line and ≥85% branch.
- [ ] `Humanizer.Analyzers.*` classes all reach ≥95% line and ≥85% branch, including `#if ROSLYN_4_14_OR_GREATER` paths.
- [ ] `Humanizer.SourceGenerators.*` internal types reach ≥90% line and ≥80% branch (error/diagnostic emission paths covered via synthetic YAML fixtures).
- [ ] Final merged report on `Humanizer` assembly: ≥95% line, ≥88% branch, ≥85% method (with FluentDate exclusion applied).
- [ ] Final merged report on `Humanizer.Analyzers` assembly: ≥95% line, ≥85% branch.
- [ ] `azure-pipelines.yml` enforces a hard gate that fails the build on regression; the gate is documented in `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md`.
- [ ] No acceptance criterion deferred; no `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]` added outside the T4-generated FluentDate and anything already committed.

## Early proof point

Task `fn-9-fill-code-coverage-gaps-toward-95-line.1` validates the fundamental approach: it adds the runsettings, rewires the T4 templates, and regenerates the baseline report. If the FluentDate exclusion does not cleanly remove those types from the merged report, or if multi-TFM merging is broken, re-evaluate the exclusion strategy (ExcludeByFile glob vs attribute; generator change vs .runsettings regex) before starting `.2+`. Every downstream task assumes the baseline numbers are honest.

## Open questions

These defaults apply unless the user speaks up.

1. **Threshold level.** Default: 95/88/85. User said "nearly 100%" — this is the honest number after excluding trivial DSL. Raise to 97/92/90 after a clean pass if desired.
2. **FluentDate DSL disposition.** Default: `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]` via T4 template edit. User said trivial getters/setters may be omitted; these DateTime factories are strictly trivial.
3. **Source generator assembly gate.** Default: in report, excluded from gate. Build-time only, not shipped.
4. **fn-8 locale overlap.** Default: hold coverage work on cs/pl/ru/ar/he/hi until fn-8.12–14 close. Epic depends on `fn-8-add-urdu-ur-locale-with-full-language`.
5. **Stryker mutation testing.** Default: out of scope for this epic; separate spec later.

## Requirement coverage

| Req | Description | Task(s) | Gap justification |
|-----|-------------|---------|-------------------|
| R1  | Runsettings + FluentDate exclusion + docs | .1 | — |
| R2  | Default fallback converter tests | .2 | — |
| R3  | Registry default-path + HeadingTable.Invariant | .3 | — |
| R4  | WordsToNumber surface + builder + SuffixScale | .4 | — |
| R5  | OrdinalDatePattern + NoMatchFoundException | .5 | — |
| R6  | Formatter branches (LocalePhraseTable, Delimited, Clitic) | .6 | — |
| R7  | Analyzer branch coverage (ROSLYN_4_14 duality + CS0029/0266) | .7 | — |
| R8  | Source generator canonical/diff/catalog diagnostic branches | .8 | — |
| R9  | Source generator engine-contract-factory dispatch | .9 | — |
| R10 | Converter tail branches (88-95% → ≥95%) | .10 | — |
| R11 | Final gate enforcement in CI + docs | .11 | Depends on .1–.10 |

tasks.spec:
## Description
Establish an honest coverage baseline and remove trivial T4-generated FluentDate DSL from the coverage target. This task is the epic's early proof point.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `coverlet.runsettings` (new, repo root) — or `tests/coverage.runsettings` if the team prefers the tests subtree
- `src/Humanizer/FluentDate/In.tt`, `In.Months.tt`, `In.SomeTimeFrom.tt`, `InDate.tt`, `InDate.Months.tt`, `InDate.SomeTimeFrom.tt`, `On.tt`, `On.Months.tt`, `On.Days.tt`, `OnDate.tt`, `OnDate.Months.tt`, `OnDate.Days.tt` (and any companion `.tt` under `src/Humanizer/FluentDate/`) — emit `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]` attribute on each generated type
- Regenerated `.cs` outputs of those templates (committed)
- `azure-pipelines.yml` — pass `--coverage-settings coverage.runsettings` (or equivalent) to `dotnet test`
- `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/adding-a-locale.md` — update `dotnet test` examples to include the runsettings flag

## Approach
- Use `dotnet-coverage` `.runsettings` regex schema (`<Sources>` and `<Attributes>`) not coverlet glob — the pipeline already runs MTP's built-in `--coverage` which invokes `dotnet-coverage`. Reference: `https://learn.microsoft.com/visualstudio/test/customizing-code-coverage-analysis`.
- Emit `[System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExcludeFromCodeCoverage(Justification = "Generated trivial DateTime factories")]` at each `public static class` in the FluentDate T4 output. Confirm `testconfig.json:9-30` already excludes the attribute.
- Keep `Humanizer.SourceGenerators` in the report but exclude from the gate threshold — the assembly is build-time-only per `CLAUDE.md`.
- Keep generated YAML tables IN the coverage target (do not undo PR #1715).

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `artifacts/ci-coverage-report/Summary.txt` — current baseline to beat
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/testconfig.json:1-60` — confirms existing attribute exclusions
- `azure-pipelines.yml:71-92` — the `DotNetCoreCLI@2` test step and `reportgenerator@5` step
- `src/Humanizer/FluentDate/*.tt` (one sample template) — T4 emission pattern
- `src/Humanizer/FluentDate/In.cs:1-20`, `In.SomeTimeFrom.cs:14-33` — shape of generated output

**Optional:**
- `https://github.com/App-vNext/Polly/blob/main/src/Polly.Core/Utils/Pipeline/DelegatingComponent.cs` — Polly precedent for excluding generated/trivial code
- `https://rjj-software.co.uk/blog/excluding-auto-generated-code-from-net-10-coverage-reports/` — gotchas with `.g.cs` path globs on .NET 10

## Key context
- Summary.txt reports 874 classes; after exclusion expect ~600 remaining.
- `Humanizer.In.*`, `Humanizer.InDate.*`, `Humanizer.On.*`, `Humanizer.OnDate.*` should disappear from the merged report.
- Do NOT use `<ExcludeFromCodeCoverage>` on any non-FluentDate type — per memory "no deferrals", every other class must be tested.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `coverlet.runsettings` (or `coverage.runsettings`) exists at a repo-stable path and is referenced from `azure-pipelines.yml`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/adding-a-locale.md`.
- [ ] `.tt` templates regenerated; every `public static class` in `src/Humanizer/FluentDate/` carries `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage(Justification = ...)]`.
- [ ] Local run of `dotnet test tests/Humanizer.Tests -- --coverage --coverage-settings <file> --coverage-output-format cobertura` produces a cobertura that omits all `Humanizer.In.*`, `Humanizer.InDate.*`, `Humanizer.On.*`, `Humanizer.OnDate.*` classes.
- [ ] `Humanizer.SourceGenerators` remains in the cobertura report (for visibility) but is marked for gate exclusion by assembly name (documented in the runsettings).
- [ ] Fresh `artifacts/ci-coverage-report/Summary.txt` captured after change shows a higher line-coverage number than the prior 91.3% baseline on the Humanizer assembly (instrumentation change alone).

## Done summary
_To be filled on completion._

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

## Description
Close the tail of `Humanizer` converters sitting at 88-95% line coverage. Each has 1-3 uncovered branches. Bundle them into one task because individually each is a few-line change with minimal file overlap and related theory-data patterns.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/ConverterTailCoverageTests.cs` (new) — consolidated branch-coverage tests, one `[Fact]` or `[Theory]` per class
- Possibly extend an existing per-locale test file where the missing branch is culture-specific (e.g. `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/fr/FrenchGenderedConverterTests.cs`)

## Target classes (all currently 88-95%)
- `Humanizer.CasingExtensions` (87.5%)
- `Humanizer.DefaultFormatter` (87.9%)
- `Humanizer.OrdinalizeExtensions` (88.2%)
- `Humanizer.Analyzers` (88.5%) — the "suppressor" utility class, not the analyzers themselves
- `Humanizer.FixedLengthTruncator` (88.8%)
- `Humanizer.DynamicNumberOfCharactersAndPreserveWordsTruncator` (89.6%)
- `Humanizer.WordFormTemplateOrdinalizer` (89.7%)
- `Humanizer.ByteSize` (89.8%)
- `Humanizer.PluralizedScaleNumberToWordsConverter` (90.3%)
- `Humanizer.VigesimalCompoundWordsToNumberConverter` (90.7%)
- `Humanizer.CompoundScaleWordsToNumberConverter` (90.8%)
- `Humanizer.GenderedNumberToWordsConverter` (90.9%)
- `Humanizer.PhraseClockNotationConverter` (91.0%)
- `Humanizer.GreedyCompoundWordsToNumberConverter` (91.1%)
- `Humanizer.LinkingAffixWordsToNumberConverter` (91.9%)
- `Humanizer.ContractedScaleWordsToNumberConverter` (92.1%)
- `Humanizer.FixedNumberOfWordsTruncator` (92.3%)
- `Humanizer.FixedNumberOfCharactersTruncator` (92.5%)
- `Humanizer.ScaleStrategyNumberToWordsConverter` (92.6%)
- `Humanizer.TerminalOrdinalScaleNumberToWordsConverter` (92.7%)
- `Humanizer.ConjunctionalScaleNumberToWordsConverter` (93.1%)
- `Humanizer.ConjoinedGenderedScaleNumberToWordsConverter` (93.2%)
- `Humanizer.SegmentedScaleNumberToWordsConverter` (93.5%)
- `Humanizer.ToQuantityExtensions` (93.5%)
- `Humanizer.HarmonyOrdinalNumberToWordsConverter` (94.3%)
- `Humanizer.WestSlavicGenderedNumberToWordsConverter` (94.5%)
- `Humanizer.DynamicLengthAndPreserveWordsTruncator` (94.8%)
- `Humanizer.AppendedGroupNumberToWordsConverter` (94.9%)
- `Humanizer.TimeSpanHumanizeExtensions` (95.0%)

## Approach
- For each target class, read the corresponding `<class>` element in `artifacts/report-merged/Cobertura.xml` and list uncovered `<line>` + `<branch>` entries. Author the smallest possible `[Fact]` or `[Theory]` input that hits each.
- Respect the "hold list" for locales owned by fn-8.12–14 (cs, pl, ru, ar, he, hi): if a target class has an uncovered branch reachable only through one of those locales, mark it in the task's completion notes and add the test AFTER fn-8 closes; otherwise prefer a locale outside the hold list.
- This task is a bundle intentionally; resist adding new test plumbing. If a class needs substantial new test infrastructure, file a new task instead of expanding scope.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `artifacts/report-merged/Cobertura.xml` — authoritative source of uncovered lines per class
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/` — pick the nearest existing test file for each target before creating a new one

**Optional:**
- `.flow/specs/fn-8-add-urdu-ur-locale-with-full-language.md` — confirm the fn-8 hold list (cs/pl/ru/ar/he/hi locales in fn-8.12-14)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Every target class listed above reaches ≥95% line and ≥90% branch in the merged report, EXCEPT any branch reachable only through cs/pl/ru/ar/he/hi — those are documented in the task summary as blocked on fn-8 completion.
- [ ] No new test files created outside `tests/Humanizer.Tests/` (reuse existing per-locale folders where applicable).
- [ ] Every new `[Theory]` asserts on a specific expected output; no `Assert.NotNull` / `Assert.DoesNotThrow` only.
- [ ] Fresh Summary.txt shows `Humanizer` assembly at ≥95% line / ≥88% branch / ≥85% method.

## Done summary
_To be filled on completion._

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

data.title: Enforce coverage gate in azure-pipelines.yml + docs

tasks.spec:
## Description
Land the hard CI coverage gate in `azure-pipelines.yml` and update contributor docs. Blocks on tasks .1–.10 — must have the honest baseline numbers in hand.

**Size:** S
**Files:**
- `azure-pipelines.yml` — add coverage threshold enforcement step
- `CLAUDE.md` — document the gate thresholds in "Quick Commands"
- `AGENTS.md` — same
- `docs/adding-a-locale.md` — contributor guidance: "new locale PR must not drop coverage below gate"
- `readme.md` (optional) — add coverage badge if a public service is wired

## Approach
- Use ReportGenerator's `MarkdownSummaryGithub` + threshold flags, OR invoke `dotnet-coverage report` with a policy file. Prefer ReportGenerator since the pipeline already runs it (`azure-pipelines.yml:82-92`).
- Gate thresholds (on the `Humanizer` assembly, per-assembly filter):
  - Line: ≥95%
  - Branch: ≥88%
  - Method: ≥85%
- Second gate on `Humanizer.Analyzers`: ≥95% line / ≥85% branch.
- Exclude `Humanizer.SourceGenerators` from the gate (keep in report).
- Two-phase rollout: land first with thresholds matching current post-.10 numbers (freeze at that point); optionally raise after a sprint of green builds.
- `dotnet test` invocations in all docs must include `--coverage-settings coverage.runsettings` (or equivalent) to match the CI invocation.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `azure-pipelines.yml:71-102` — current test + reportgenerator + fail-on-issue wiring
- `CLAUDE.md:Quick Commands` section
- `AGENTS.md:29` — `dotnet test` invocation
- `docs/adding-a-locale.md:392-394` — the canonical `dotnet test` block
- Final `artifacts/ci-coverage-report/Summary.txt` produced after .10 completes — source of truth for gate numbers

**Optional:**
- `https://reportgenerator.io/usage` — threshold flag reference
- `https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/additional-tools/dotnet-coverage` — `report` subcommand with policy

## Acceptance
- [ ] `azure-pipelines.yml` fails the build when line coverage on `Humanizer` falls below the gate (verified by a smoke commit that lowers coverage intentionally, then reverts).
- [ ] `Humanizer.Analyzers` gate is enforced separately.
- [ ] `Humanizer.SourceGenerators` is excluded from the gate but still reported.
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/adding-a-locale.md` all reference the runsettings path and cite the gate thresholds in one place.
- [ ] No `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]` or `.runsettings` exclusion was added beyond what .1 introduced.
- [ ] Merged report matches or exceeds: Humanizer ≥95/88/85, Analyzers ≥95/85/—.

## Done summary
_To be filled on completion._

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

## Description
Add real tests for the three `Default*` fallback converters that currently sit at 0% line coverage. They are the registry's safety net when no locale-specific converter is registered.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/Default/DefaultDateToOrdinalWordConverterTests.cs` (new)
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/Default/DefaultDateOnlyToOrdinalWordConverterTests.cs` (new, guarded by `NET6_0_OR_GREATER` if needed)
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/Default/DefaultNumberToWordsConverterTests.cs` (new)

## Approach
- Instantiate each converter directly (they are `internal` but `InternalsVisibleTo` is already set for `Humanizer.Tests`). Assert on return values — do not route through the `Configurator` registry for this task (that is covered in `.3`).
- Exercise `DefaultDateToOrdinalWordConverter.Convert(DateTime, CultureInfo, GrammaticalCase)` and its `DateOnly` twin with:
  - An English culture (`en-US`) — English branch, no mark stripping
  - An Arabic culture (`ar-SA`) — non-English branch, covers LTR/RTL/ALM stripping (characters `\u200E`, `\u200F`, `\u061C`)
  - A culture whose `ToString("d")` embeds a directional mark (Hebrew `he-IL`, Persian `fa-IR`) — verify the stripper produces bidi-clean output
- `DefaultNumberToWordsConverter.Convert(long, ...)` and `.ConvertToOrdinal(int, ...)` — both overloads, at least one numeric value per call.
- Use `UseCulture` attribute OR pass `CultureInfo` explicitly — prefer explicit arguments to avoid thread-state leaks.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `src/Humanizer/Localisation/DateToOrdinalWords/DefaultDateToOrdinalWordConverter.cs:10-50` — branches
- `src/Humanizer/Localisation/DateToOrdinalWords/DefaultDateOnlyToOrdinalWordConverter.cs:12-52`
- `src/Humanizer/Localisation/NumberToWords/DefaultNumberToWordsConverter.cs:11-30`
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/OrdinalizerRegistryRecoveryTests.cs` — precedent for direct-instantiation tests
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/UseCultureAttribute.cs:22-54` — culture scoping pattern

## Acceptance
- [ ] `DefaultDateToOrdinalWordConverter` reaches ≥95% line and ≥90% branch coverage (covers `SanitizeNonEnglishDate` and every mark-stripping code point).
- [ ] `DefaultDateOnlyToOrdinalWordConverter` reaches same thresholds under `NET6_0_OR_GREATER`.
- [ ] `DefaultNumberToWordsConverter` reaches 100% line coverage (both `Convert(long)` and `ConvertToOrdinal(int)` overloads asserted).
- [ ] New tests follow `UseCultureAttribute` pattern and pass on net10.0, net8.0; net48 behavior matches when run on Windows.
- [ ] No new `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]` attributes introduced.

## Done summary
_To be filled on completion._

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

## Description
Cover the three 50%-line converter registries and the `HeadingTableCatalog.Invariant` fallback path. Currently the registry constructors are touched by static `Configurator` init but their default-factory lambdas (`_ => new DefaultX()`) never fire because every shipping locale registers an override. A test that passes an unknown culture exercises the lambda.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/Default/RegistryFallbackTests.cs` (new) — exercises `DateToOrdinalWordsConverterRegistry`, `DateOnlyToOrdinalWordsConverterRegistry`, `TimeOnlyToClockNotationConvertersRegistry`, `DateToOrdinalWordsExtensions`
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/HeadingTableInvariantFallbackTests.cs` (new) — forces the `HeadingTableCatalog.Invariant` code path

## Approach
- For each registry, call the public extension method (`date.ToOrdinalWords(culture)`, `timeOnly.ToClockNotation(...)`) under `UseCulture("xx-ZZ")` (real BCP-47 with no registered converter). Validate the result matches the `Default*` output. This walks through `LocaliserRegistry<T>.ResolveForCulture` → default-factory → `Default*` converter — hits both the registry gap and the fallback converter dispatch branches.
- For `HeadingTableCatalog.Invariant`: the nested static type at `src/Humanizer/HeadingTable.cs:55-60` holds an invariant-culture cache. Reach it by calling `heading.ToHeading(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)`. The existing `HeadingTests.ToHeadingFallsBackToEnglishForUnknownCultures` (line 208) uses `eo` which hits `ResolveCore("en")` — different path. Use `CultureInfo.InvariantCulture` or a culture that actually skips `en` in its parent chain.
- If after a culture-invariant call the `invariantCache` path is still unreachable from the public surface, file a cleanup note in the epic spec. Do NOT add `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]`.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `src/Humanizer/Configuration/DateToOrdinalWordsConverterRegistry.cs:3-8`
- `src/Humanizer/Configuration/DateOnlyToOrdinalWordsConverterRegistry.cs:4-8`
- `src/Humanizer/Configuration/TimeOnlyToClockNotationConvertersRegistry.cs:5-9`
- `src/Humanizer/DateToOrdinalWordsExtensions.cs` — extension methods that dispatch
- `src/Humanizer/HeadingTable.cs:53-60` — nested `invariantCache` static
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/HeadingTests.cs:208` — existing fallback test

**Optional:**
- `src/Humanizer/Configuration/LocaliserRegistry.cs` — how default-factory is invoked

## Acceptance
- [ ] All four 50%-coverage registries and extension entry points reach ≥95% line and ≥90% branch coverage.
- [ ] `HeadingTableCatalog.Invariant` / `invariantCache` reaches ≥80% line coverage via a real test (no `[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]`).
- [ ] Tests use `UseCultureAttribute` or explicit `CultureInfo.InvariantCulture` and restore thread state cleanly.
- [ ] No registry state is mutated mid-test (no `Configurator.X.Register`/`Unregister` calls).

## Done summary
_To be filled on completion._

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

data.title: WordsToNumber surface + ordinal-map builder + SuffixScale branches

tasks.spec:
## Description
Close gaps in `WordsToNumberExtension` `Try*` overloads, `TokenMapWordsToNumberOrdinalMapBuilder` (0%), and `SuffixScaleWordsToNumberConverter` (52%). All three are genuine-logic holes.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/WordsToNumberTryTests.cs` (new) — Try-overload coverage
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/TokenMapWordsToNumberOrdinalMapBuilderTests.cs` (new)
- `tests/Humanizer.Tests/Localisation/tr/SuffixScaleWordsToNumberConverterTests.cs` (new or extend existing tr test file)

## Approach
- **WordsToNumberExtension Try overloads.** Cover both `TryToNumber(string, out long, CultureInfo)` (`src/Humanizer/WordsToNumberExtension.cs:54-55`) and `TryToNumber(string, out long, out string? unrecognizedWord, CultureInfo)` (`:79-80`). Minimum cases: (a) valid input returning `true`, (b) invalid input returning `false` with `unrecognizedWord` populated, (c) null/empty input returning `false`.
- **TokenMapWordsToNumberOrdinalMapBuilder.** Call both public `Build` overloads: one accepting `string numberToWordsKind`, one accepting `INumberToWordsConverter`. Use a simple locale with ordinal word forms (en is the easiest). Verify the returned map contains expected entries for small ordinals (1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th).
- **SuffixScaleWordsToNumberConverter.** Tr culture. Read `src/Humanizer/Localisation/WordsToNumber/SuffixScaleWordsToNumberConverter.cs:32-35` (empty-input throw), `:46-56` (negative-prefix loop), `:100-112` (lookahead for bare-scale compounds), `:191-220` (tens/teens suffix). Author tr inputs that hit each branch — use the Cobertura line numbers for exactness.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `src/Humanizer/WordsToNumberExtension.cs:1-120` — all overloads + `out` parameter shapes
- `src/Humanizer/Localisation/WordsToNumber/TokenMapWordsToNumberOrdinalMapBuilder.cs:1-81`
- `src/Humanizer/Localisation/WordsToNumber/SuffixScaleWordsToNumberConverter.cs:1-230`

For each pair, assert both "phrase found → returns true, populates out" and "phrase not found → returns false, out = default". Exercise the tail `ResolveCore("en")` path in `LocalePhraseTableCatalog.Resolve` (`:148-169`) with a culture whose ancestry walks past invariant back to English.

- **CliticCollectionFormatter.** Cover `count switch { 0 => "", 1 => lastItem!, _ => ... }` (all three arms), null guards. `src/Humanizer/Localisation/CollectionFormatters/CliticCollectionFormatter.cs:22-73`.

data.title: Analyzer branch coverage: code-fix providers + ROSLYN_4_14 duality

tasks.spec:
## Description
Close analyzer branch gaps. `WordsToNumberMigrationCodeFixProvider` (82.2% line / ~40.6% branch), `NamespaceMigrationCodeFixProvider` (88.3% line / ~69.2% branch), `NamespaceMigrationAnalyzer` (92.8% line / ~80% branch). Two sources of low branch coverage: CS0029 vs CS0266 matrix and the `#if ROSLYN_4_14_OR_GREATER` conditional compilation fork.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/WordsToNumberMigrationCodeFixTests.cs` (extend — add tests for CS0029 arg path, `IWordsToNumberConverter` path, non-fix bailouts)
- `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/NamespaceMigrationCodeFixTests.cs` (extend — add qualified-name replacement + `GetReplacementName` edge)
- `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/NamespaceMigrationAnalyzerTests.cs` (extend — add `AnalyzeQualifiedName` parent-skip branch)
- Possibly `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests.csproj` — multi-target Roslyn to exercise both `ROSLYN_4_14_OR_GREATER` arms if not already

## Approach
- Use the existing `CSharpAnalyzerTest<,>` and `CSharpCodeFixTest<,,>` (or v3 variant) pattern in `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/Verifiers.cs` (51 lines).
- **WordsToNumberMigrationCodeFixProvider.** Test matrix:
  - `CS0266` → int local assignment (existing)
  - `CS0029` → method argument requiring int
  - Input with `IWordsToNumberConverter.Convert(...)` direct call (`AllInterfaces.Any` path at `:77-78`)
  - Negative case: explicit cast already present (provider bails)
  - Negative case: non-long return (no fix applicable)
  - Null `root` / null `semanticModel` branches (`:24`, `:28`, `:83`) — exercise via malformed input
- **NamespaceMigrationCodeFixProvider.** Test:
  - Qualified-name replacement (`:89-109`)
  - `GetReplacementName` where `fullName.Length == matchedNamespace.Length` (`:137`)
  - Both `ROSLYN_4_14_OR_GREATER` arms (`:111-133`, `:149-164`) via multi-target or two test csproj configurations
- **NamespaceMigrationAnalyzer.** `AnalyzeQualifiedName` `parentQualified` skip-outer branch (`:88-93`).

Use markup syntax `{|DIAG001:token|}` on test code for expected diagnostic locations. Use `CodeActionIndex` for multi-fix scenarios.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `src/Humanizer.Analyzers/WordsToNumberMigrationCodeFixProvider.cs:17-96`
- `src/Humanizer.Analyzers/NamespaceMigrationCodeFixProvider.cs:37-178`
- `src/Humanizer.Analyzers/NamespaceMigrationAnalyzer.cs:75-168`
- `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/Verifiers.cs` — verifier setup
- `https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-sdk/tree/main/src/Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Testing` — patterns for `CodeActionIndex` and multi-fix tests

**Optional:**
- `tests/Humanizer.Analyzers.Tests/NamespaceMigrationAnalyzerTests.cs`, `NamespaceMigrationCodeFixTests.cs`, `WordsToNumberMigrationCodeFixTests.cs` — existing patterns

## Acceptance
- [ ] All three analyzer classes reach ≥95% line and ≥85% branch in the merged report.
- [ ] Both `ROSLYN_4_14_OR_GREATER` arms are exercised (either via multi-target test config or documented test matrix run).
- [ ] Negative tests exist for every diagnostic-producing branch (code that should NOT trigger a diagnostic).
- [ ] Non-applicable fix scenarios are covered (provider bails with no edits).

data.title: Source generator diagnostics: canonical diff + YAML catalog error paths

tasks.spec:
## Description
Cover source-generator diagnostic and error-emission branches for the canonical-locale engine. Classes at 70-88% line are `LocaleSemanticDiff` (72.6%), `CanonicalLocaleAuthoring` (80.1%), `LocaleCatalogInput` (75.5%), `LocaleDefinitionFile` (73.3%). All are `Humanizer.SourceGenerators` internals that only light up when YAML input exercises specific malformed/unknown-key/conflict paths — shipping locales do not hit them.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/Fixtures/` (new) — synthetic YAML fixtures, one file per diagnostic code path (e.g. `missing_required_field.yml`, `unknown_key.yml`, `conflicting_profile.yml`, `malformed_scalar.yml`, `diff_kind_added.yml`, etc.)
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/SourceGenerators/LocaleDiagnosticFixturesTests.cs` (new) — drives the generator with each fixture and asserts the emitted diagnostic / diff kind
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/Fixtures/Fixtures.csproj` additions if embedding fixtures as resources

## Approach
- Follow the existing pattern in `HumanizerSourceGeneratorTests.cs:1-1561` (1561 lines): `CSharpGeneratorDriver`, in-memory `AdditionalText` for YAML input, run the generator, inspect `GeneratorDriverRunResult`.
- One `[Theory]` per fixture file with `[MemberData]` enumerating the fixtures. Assert on expected diagnostic ID(s) and/or on the `LocaleSemanticDiff` kind emitted.
- Cover each unique branch in:
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Common/CanonicalLocaleAuthoring.cs:LocaleSemanticDiff` (complexity 80)
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Common/CanonicalLocaleAuthoring.cs:CanonicalLocaleAuthoring` (complexity 173)
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Common/LocaleYamlCatalog.cs:LocaleCatalogInput` (complexity 268)
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Common/LocaleYamlCatalog.cs:LocaleDefinitionFile`
- Each fixture should be minimal (a handful of lines of YAML) and target ONE branch. Name the fixture file after the branch it covers.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Common/CanonicalLocaleAuthoring.cs` (full file)
- `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Common/LocaleYamlCatalog.cs` (full file)
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/SourceGenerators/HumanizerSourceGeneratorTests.cs:1-400` — existing driver setup pattern
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/SourceGenerators/CanonicalLocaleSchemaTests.cs:1-100` — existing schema-test pattern

**Optional:**
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/SourceGenerators/LocalePhraseNormalizationTests.cs` — how normalization tests are structured

## Acceptance
- [ ] `LocaleSemanticDiff` reaches ≥90% line and ≥80% branch.
- [ ] `CanonicalLocaleAuthoring` reaches ≥90% line and ≥80% branch.
- [ ] `LocaleCatalogInput` reaches ≥90% line and ≥80% branch.
- [ ] `LocaleDefinitionFile` reaches ≥90% line and ≥80% branch.
- [ ] Each new fixture file targets one documented branch; filename matches the branch description.
- [ ] No modification to any shipped YAML in `src/Humanizer/Locales/`.

## Description
Cover the engine-contract-factory dispatch branches in `Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Generators.ProfileCatalogs`. `WordsToNumberEngineContractFactory` sits at 77.3% line / ~45.4% branch and `TimeOnlyToClockNotationEngineContractFactory` at 82.0% / ~71.4%; `NumberToWordsEngineContractFactory` at 88.1% also needs a top-up. Low branch coverage indicates many `else if (kind == "X")` dispatch arms that no shipping locale uses.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/Fixtures/EngineContracts/` (new) — one synthetic YAML fixture per engine-contract kind
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/SourceGenerators/EngineContractFactoryTests.cs` (new)

## Approach
- Enumerate every `else if (kind == "X")` arm in:
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Generators/ProfileCatalogs/WordsToNumberEngineContractFactory.cs`
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Generators/ProfileCatalogs/TimeOnlyToClockNotationEngineContractFactory.cs`
  - `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Generators/ProfileCatalogs/NumberToWordsEngineContractFactory.cs`
- For each arm, create a minimal synthetic locale YAML fixture that declares only what's needed to route through that arm and assert the generator emits the expected `EngineContract`.
- Also cover negative paths: unknown kind (`else` / default branch) must emit a diagnostic; verify via `GeneratorDriverRunResult.Diagnostics`.

## Investigation targets
**Required:**
- `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Generators/ProfileCatalogs/WordsToNumberEngineContractFactory.cs` (full file, ~complexity 135)
- `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Generators/ProfileCatalogs/TimeOnlyToClockNotationEngineContractFactory.cs`
- `src/Humanizer.SourceGenerators/Generators/ProfileCatalogs/NumberToWordsEngineContractFactory.cs`
- `src/Humanizer/Locales/en.yml`, `src/Humanizer/Locales/ja.yml` — reference YAML shapes for number engines
- `tests/Humanizer.SourceGenerators.Tests/SourceGenerators/HumanizerSourceGeneratorTests.cs` — driver pattern

**Optional:**
- `src/Humanizer/Locales/th.yml`, `src/Humanizer/Locales/vi.yml` — clock-notation engine shapes

## Acceptance
- [ ] `WordsToNumberEngineContractFactory` reaches ≥90% line and ≥80% branch.
- [ ] `TimeOnlyToClockNotationEngineContractFactory` reaches ≥90% line and ≥80% branch.
- [ ] `NumberToWordsEngineContractFactory` reaches ≥95% line and ≥85% branch.
- [ ] Every `kind == "X"` arm has at least one test fixture; the "unknown kind" default emits a diagnostic.
- [ ] Test fixtures are minimal (≤30 lines of YAML each) and checked into the fixtures directory, not inlined in C# strings.
